Describe the Multidisciplinary Rules rule for rules[66].

Q&A 6.2: An out of domain or non-coverage result from one of the two (Q)SAR models requires additional assessment to classify the compound as a Class 5 impurity. Expert review may include one or a combination of: (1) Comparison to structurally similar analogs for which bacterial reverse mutation assay data are available (read-across approach); (2) Expert review of the chemical structure to determine if there is potential for the chemical to react with DNA; (3) (Q)SAR output from an additional validated model of the same methodology that generates a prediction within its applicability domain.

Describe the ICH E Guidelines rule for The main deliverable is a harmonized guideline on.

The main deliverable is a harmonized guideline on adaptive clinical trials, which will provide clarity on: (a) A common terminology for adaptive clinical trials, (b) The potential benefits of adaptive clinical trials and areas of meaningful applications, (c) The principles for the design, conduct, analysis, and proper interpretation of adaptive clinical trials, including considerations of the risk of erroneous conclusions (control of false positive and false negative conclusions, and reliability of effect estimates), maintenance of trial integrity, and handling of operational challenges, (d) The documentation that is important for the planning and implementation of adaptive clinical trials and the interactions between sponsors and regulatory agencies.

What does ICH S11 — Nonclinical Safety Testing in Support of Development of Paediatric Pha state verbatim about species selection?

"When a JAS is warranted, in most cases a single species is considered sufficient. In principle, the same species as used in adult repeated-dose studies should initially be considered as the species for a JAS, preferably a rodent. In all cases, the selected species should be justified, as nonclinical studies in a pharmacologically non-relevant species can give rise to misinterpretation and are not recommended."

Context: When a JAS is warranted, in most cases a single species is considered sufficient. The same species as used in adult repeated-dose studies should initially be considered, preferably a rodent. Nonclinic

According to ICH Safety Guidelines S1A, S1B(R1), S1C(R2), S2(R1), S3A, S3B, S4, S5(R3), S8 —, what must sponsors do regarding Part II (Addendum), Section 2 — Weight of Evidence Factors?

A weight-of-evidence (WoE) approach may be used to determine whether the standard 2-year rat carcinogenicity study is needed. This WoE approach evaluates six factors: (1) genotoxicity data, (2) relevant animal and human data, (3) target of pharmacological activity and relevance to carcinogenicity, (4) findings from general toxicology studies, (5) study of structure-activity relationships, and (6) toxicokinetic and mechanistic data.

Source: Part II (Addendum), Section 2 — Weight of Evidence Factors

Per ICH E Guidelines, what is the requirement for The execution of an interim analysis should be?

The execution of an interim analysis should be a completely confidential process. All staff involved in the conduct of the trial should remain blind to the results of such analyses because of the possibility that their attitudes to the trial will be modified and cause changes in the characteristics of patients to be recruited or biases in treatment comparisons.

What is the regulatory requirement under Multidisciplinary Rules for rules[40]?

For a potential degradation product that has been characterized as mutagenic, it is important to understand if the degradation pathway is relevant to the drug substance and drug product manufacturing processes and/or their proposed packaging and storage conditions. A well-designed accelerated stability study (e.g., 40°C/75% relative humidity, 6 months) in the proposed packaging, with appropriate analytical procedures is recommended to determine the relevance of the potential degradation product. Based on the result of these accelerated studies, if it is anticipated that the degradation product will form at levels approaching the acceptable limit under the proposed packaging and storage conditions, then efforts to control formation of the degradation product is expected. In these cases, monitoring for the drug substance or drug product degradation product in long term primary stability studies at the proposed storage conditions (in the proposed commercial pack) is expected unless otherwise justified.

Describe the ICH E Guidelines rule for recommended.

Interpretation of trial results should focus on the main estimator for each agreed estimand providing that the corresponding estimate is verified to be robust through the sensitivity analysis. Supplementary analyses for an estimand can be conducted in addition to the main and sensitivity analysis to provide additional insights into the understanding of the treatment effect. They generally play a lesser role for interpretation of trial results. Where the primary estimand(s) of interest is agreed between sponsor and regulator, and the sensitivity analysis verifies the estimate is reliable, supplementary analyses should generally be given lower priority in assessment.

What is the regulatory requirement under Final 4 Verified for Toxicity assessment; Section 4.1 — Basic toxicity assessment?

The WHO guidelines on nonclinical evaluation of vaccines (WHO Technical Report Series No. 927, Annex 1, 2005) do not include a dedicated safety pharmacology section. There is no distinct safety pharmacology requirement for vaccines. Nonclinical safety is addressed through Section 4 (Toxicity assessment): Section 4.1 (Basic toxicity assessment) specifies that toxicity studies must identify potential toxic effects on target organs, assess dose, route(s) of exposure, duration, frequency, and potential reversibility. Section 4.1.4 (Parameters monitored) requires studies to address systemic toxicity and effects on the immune system. Safety pharmacology as a standalone concept (cardiovascular, respiratory, CNS effects per ICH S7A) is not referenced or required for vaccines under this guideline.

Source: Section 4 — Toxicity assessment; Section 4.1 — Basic toxicity assessment; Section 4.1.4 — Parameters monitored

According to Final 4 Verified, what must sponsors do regarding Section III.B.5 — Toxicology Studies (Secondary consideratio?

The FDA guidance does not require standalone safety pharmacology studies for investigational cellular and gene therapy (CGT) products. Safety pharmacology assessment is incorporated within the comprehensive toxicology study design. Section III.B.5 (Toxicology Studies) requires that the safety assessment be 'sufficiently comprehensive to permit identification, characterization, and quantification of potential local and systemic toxicities.' Functional safety parameters — including behavioral testing, neurological exams, ophthalmic exams, and cardiac assessments — are listed as 'additional parameters specific to either the investigational CGT product used and/or specific to the intended patient population' (Section III.B.5.b.viii). The data collected should include both morphological and functional assessment, whenever possible.

Source: Section III.B.5 — Toxicology Studies (Secondary considerations for toxicology study design, item viii)
